title: Oscars 2026: All 10 Best Picture Nominees, Ranked from Worst to Best
text: Kate Erbland and Ryan Lattanzio provide full rankings of the 2026 Best Picture nominees. Bugonia (rank 10): Yorgos Lanthimos' thriller features Jesse Plemons as a conspiracy-minded beekeeper who kidnaps a pharmaceutical executive (Emma Stone). Critics note the film feels like a repetition of Lanthimos' familiar aesthetic. F1 (rank 9): Joseph Kosinski's racing drama starring Brad Pitt received nominations for editing, visual effects, and sound. While acknowledged as entertaining, reviewers felt it functions primarily as a blockbuster placeholder. Frankenstein (rank 8): Guillermo del Toro's Netflix adaptation emphasizes practical effects over CGI. Critics express disappointment that the film lacks del Toro's signature darkness. Sentimental Value (rank 7): Joachim Trier's family drama reunites him with actress Renate Reinsve. The film explores emotional damage through performances from Stellan Skarsgård and Elle Fanning. Marty Supreme (rank 6): Timothée Chalamet plays an unlikable protagonist in this 1950s-set drama about ambition and manipulation. Hamnet (rank 5): Chloé Zhao's Shakespeare-inspired film centers on the family's grief following their son's death. Train Dreams (rank 4): Clint Bentley's meditation on mortality features Joel Edgerton in a contemplative performance. Sinners (rank 3): Ryan Coogler's vampire thriller earned 16 Oscar nominations with Michael B. Jordan delivering dual performances. One Battle After Another (rank 2): Paul Thomas Anderson's paranoid comedy-thriller addresses contemporary political anxieties. The Secret Agent (rank 1): Kleber Mendonça Filho's Brazilian film features Wagner Moura as a professor navigating 1977 fascist Brazil.
